body: As part of the **Binius in ArkLib** grant, the Binius formalization has been merged but relies on several unproven `Prop` typeclasses acting as residual axioms (e.g., `FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual`, `Prop4212Case1Residual`, `PreTensorCombineMultilinearResidual`, `FoldPreTensorCombineAffineSplitResidual`, etc.). This issue tracks discharging these residuals with actual proofs.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body: Current-repo audit cross-reference: this is the right theme. I filed a more detailed Binius grant-closeout tracker at #313 with the focused validation failure (`BBFSmallFieldIOPCS.lean` blocked by missing `BinaryBasefold/General.olean`) and the observed residual surfaces (`ExtractMLPCorrectnessResidual`, `FoldPreservesBBFCodeMembershipResidual`, `FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual`, `FinalSumcheckStepLogicCompleteResidual`, and role-named completeness/RBR assumptions).

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
Crux brick landed (`c4b5352e4`): **`baseFoldMatrix_det_ne_zero`** (`BinaryBasefold/BaseFoldDetBrick.lean`, 0 sorry, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`).

- `qMap_total_fiber_one_sub`: the two single-step qMap fiber points differ by exactly the first source-domain basis vector — `x₁ − x₀ = basis_x 0` (via the closed fiber form `qMap_total_fiber_one_level_eq`).
- `baseFoldMatrix_det`: `det [[x₁, −x₀], [−1, 1]] = x₁ − x₀ = basis_x 0` in `L`.
- Nonsingularity follows (basis vectors nonzero; subspace coercion injective), within the `destIdx ≤ ℓ` range `FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual` demands.

**Remaining for the full residual** (worked out, mechanical): the recursive block factorization `foldMatrixNat (n+1) ≅ fromBlocks (x₁•1) (−x₀•1) (−1•1) (1•1) ⬝ fromBlocks M₀ 0 0 M₁` (block products verified by hand against the definition's bit-indexing: rows `(a%2, a/2)`, cols `(b/2^n, b%2^n)`), giving `det = ±(x₁−x₀)^{2^n}·det M₀·det M₁`, then induction on `steps` with this brick at each level. The other three residuals (`PreTensorCombineMultilinearResidual`, `FoldPreTensorCombineAffineSplitResidual`, `Prop4212Case1Residual`) are independent and unstarted.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body: Second brick landed (`4d92ec755`): **`FoldDetSplit.lean`** — the `rowSplit`/`colSplit` index equivalences (matching `foldMatrixNat`'s bit-conventions) + **`detSplitFactor`**: `det (fromBlocks (x_b•M) ((−x_a)•N) ((−1)•M) N) = (x_b−x_a)^m · det M · det N`. Combined with `baseFoldMatrix_det_ne_zero` (previous brick), the only step left for `FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual` is the induction welding `foldMatrixNat (n+1)` to the block form through the two equivs.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body: **Lane claim (~09:00Z):** taking the final `FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual` induction welding on top of the two landed bricks (`baseFoldMatrix_det_ne_zero`, `detSplitFactor` + `rowSplit`/`colSplit`). Plan, in `FoldDetSplit.lean`: (1) `foldMatrixNat_succ_eq` — the recursion at `n+1` IS `(fromBlocks ((x₁:L)•M₀) ((−x₀:L)•M₁) ((−1)•M₀) M₁).submatrix (rowSplit n) (colSplit n)` (entrywise: low row bit ↔ base-matrix row, high column bit ↔ base-matrix column = fiber choice, `b%2ⁿ = b−2ⁿ` on the high block); (2) `foldMatrixNat_det_ne_zero` by induction via `det_submatrix_equiv_ne_zero` + `detSplitFactor` + the fiber-separation coercion `(x₁:L)−(x₀:L) = (basis_x 0 : L) ≠ 0`; (3) the `FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual` instance (`foldMatrix` is definitionally `foldMatrixNat` at the lifted point). Will report with build + axiom audit.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
## ✅ `FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual` DISCHARGED (`1ffa92a03`)

`BinaryBasefold/FoldDetDischarge.lean` (0 sorry, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`):
- **`foldMatrixNat_succ_eq_submatrix`** — the recursion *is* the `rowSplit`/`colSplit` reindexing of `fromBlocks (x₁•M₀) ((−x₀)•M₁) ((−1)•M₀) M₁` (16-way ext case analysis; the dependent-motive pitfalls were the real fight: `split_ifs` for the column dite whose branch uses its own proof, and `Nat.lt_of_lt_of_eq … (pow_succ 2 n)` composition instead of in-place `rw [pow_succ] at` on `Fin.isLt`).
- **`foldMatrixNat_det_ne_zero`** — induction: `det = (x₁−x₀)^{2^n} · det M₀ · det M₁` (via `detSplitFactor` + `det_submatrix_equiv_ne_zero`), base nonzero by fiber separation `x₁ − x₀ = basis_x 0`.
- **`instance instFoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual`** — the residual class discharged for its `destIdx ≤ ℓ` range.

Together with the two earlier bricks this fully removes the AdditiveNTT matrix-invertibility residual from the Binius soundness layer. Remaining on this issue: `PreTensorCombineMultilinearResidual`, `FoldPreTensorCombineAffineSplitResidual`, `Prop4212Case1Residual`.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
Lane claim (~10:02Z): taking the `PreTensorCombineMultilinearResidual` tensor-combine multilinearity lane, with the nearby `FoldPreTensorCombineAffineSplitResidual` bridge as the first brick if needed. I read the latest thread: `FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual` is already discharged in `FoldDetDischarge.lean`, so I will not duplicate that.

Plan is to work in scratch against `Soundness/Incremental.lean`: (1) prove exact row/even-odd identities for `preTensorCombine_WordStack`; (2) weld one-fold affine split; (3) use induction over `steps` to replace the residual instance. I will report build/axiom status before pushing.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
## ✅ `FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual` DISCHARGED (`51d4732e9`)

The induction welding is landed in `FoldDetSplit.lean` on top of the two bricks — the residual now has a global **instance**, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, 0 sorry, full-file build exit 0:

- **`foldMatrixNat_succ_eq`** — the welding identity: one step of the recursion IS `fromBlocks (x₁•M₀) ((−x₀)•M₁) ((−1)•M₀) M₁` reindexed by `rowSplit`/`colSplit` (low row bit ↔ base-matrix row; high column bit ↔ base-matrix column = fiber choice; `b % 2ⁿ = b − 2ⁿ` on the high block). Four entry cases, each ending in a single `rfl` through the recursion matcher.
- **`foldZ_sub_ne_zero`** — fiber separation transported to `L`: `x₁ − x₀ = (basis_x 0 : L) ≠ 0` via `qMap_total_fiber_one_sub` + `AddSubgroupClass.coe_sub` + basis nonvanishing.
- **`foldMatrixNat_det_ne_zero`** — the induction: `det_submatrix_equiv_ne_zero` + `detSplitFactor` give `det = ±(x₁−x₀)^{2ⁿ}·det M₀·det M₁` at each level; base case via a manual `Unique (Fin (2^0))` + `Matrix.det_unique`.
- **`instFoldMatrixDetNeZero`** — the instance: `foldMatrix` is definitionally `foldMatrixNat` at the lifted point; the `destIdx ≤ ℓ` hypothesis supplies the `≤ ℓ` range.

**Workflow gotcha for the fleet** (cost a long detour): in this section's binder positions, write Fin indices as `i.val + …`, **never** `↑i + …`. The coercion-arrow form makes every downstream `isDefEq` through the `sDomain` subtype lifts diverge (observed >16M heartbeats comparing *syntactically identical* `qMap_total_fiber` applications; with `i.val` the same checks pass at default budget). `Fin.val`-of-mk is also opaque to `omega` — normalize with `show` first. The 16M heartbeat budget on the welding identity covers the four per-entry matcher `rfl`s and is in-file (survives autosync).

**Remaining for this issue**: `PreTensorCombineMultilinearResidual`, `FoldPreTensorCombineAffineSplitResidual`, `Prop4212Case1Residual` — independent, unstarted. Consumers in `Soundness/Proposition4_21.lean` can now drop their `[FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual …]` hypotheses wherever `FoldDetSplit` is imported (cleanup tracked in #313).

comments.author: lalalune
lalalune
comments.body:
## Analysis: `Prop4212Case1Residual` is (likely) UNPROVABLE as stated — degenerate disagreement surface

While building the discharge for the other two residuals I traced the event algebra of `Prop4212Case1Residual` and hit a wall that is mathematical, not mechanical. Recording it so nobody burns a session grinding the probability bound directly.

**The degeneracy.** `fiberwiseDisagreementSet` (`Code.lean`) at `steps ≠ 0` is
`Finset.univ.filter fun _y => ∃ x, f x ≠ g x` — the predicate **ignores the quotient point** `_y`. So the set is `∅` (if `f = g`) or `univ` (otherwise); it never expresses "the fibers over `y` on which `f, g` disagree".

**Consequence for Case 1.** Unfolding `incrementalFoldingBadEvent` under the block-close branch with this surface, the joint event `¬E(i,k) ∧ E(i,k+1)` becomes (writing `h := fold_k f − fold_k f̄`):
- `k+1 < ϑ`: `h ≠ 0 ∧ fold(h, r_new) = 0` (everywhere) — fine, this is ≤ the single-point SZ event and the bound holds.
- `k+1 = ϑ` (the `foldingBadEvent` case): `h ≠ 0 ∧ ∃ y, fold(h, r_new)(y) = 0` — an **existential over all of `S^{destIdx}`**, because `Δ_fiber = univ` instead of the honest disagreeing-fiber set. For any `y` whose fiber satisfies `h ≡ 0` (possible with `h ≠ 0` globally), the per-`y` butterfly polynomial in `r_new` is **identically zero**, so the event holds for *every* challenge and the probability is `1 > |S^{destIdx}|/|L|`. The residual is ∀-quantified over `f`, so as stated it is false in any configuration admitting such an `f` (the paper's argument only sums over *disagreeing* fibers, where the polynomial is provably nonzero via the now-proven fold-matrix nonsingularity).

**The honest fix** (matches the residual docstring's "reworking the disagreement-set witness extraction"): redefine the `steps ≠ 0` branch as the per-fiber set
`univ.filter fun y => ∃ c : Fin (2^steps), f (qMap_total_fiber … y c) ≠ g (qMap_total_fiber … y c)`,
then Case 1 is the textbook per-fiber Schwartz–Zippel (degree ≤ 1, nonzero by `baseFoldMatrix_det_ne_zero`) + union bound over `Δ_fiber ⊆ S^{destIdx}`. Blast radius: 44 use sites across `Code`/`Compliance`/`Soundness/{Incremental, Proposition4_21, FoldDistance, Lift, QueryPhaseSoundness}` — I'm taking this refactor next unless someone is already in those files.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
Small stabilization brick pushed: `1e22802b3` (`fix(#317): stabilize fold input linearity helpers`). This keeps the single-step preTensor-combine lane green after the surrounding index changes by making the fold input linearity helpers carry the source-successor bound explicitly and avoiding the dependent rewrite failure around `destIdx`.

Validation run: `lake env lean ArkLib/ProofSystem/Binius/BinaryBasefold/Soundness/Incremental.lean` exits 0. The axiom audit lines for the existing residual wrappers remain at the standard `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

Remaining on this thread is unchanged: full `PreTensorCombineMultilinearResidual`, the general `FoldPreTensorCombineAffineSplitResidual`, and the larger `Prop4212Case1Residual` disagreement-set refactor.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
Codex residual-bridge refresh (2026-06-10):

`Soundness/Incremental.lean` now has useful one-step tensor/fold bridge material in the working tree:

- `preTensorCombine_step1_row_zero` / `preTensorCombine_step1_row_one`: identify the two rows of `preTensorCombine_WordStack ... steps = 1` with folds at challenges `0` and `1`.
- `fold_eq_affine_binary_challenges`: pointwise affine form of a single fold between those two binary challenge rows.
- `fold_eq_multilinearCombine_preTensorCombine_step1`: packages the previous two into the `multilinearCombine` form consumed later by the pre-tensor residual path.

This looks like the right local bridge for the `PreTensorCombineMultilinearResidual` / affine-split family: specialize the general residual to `steps = 1`, prove the row projections first, then lift through `multilinearCombine` rather than expanding the full tensor stack in one proof.

Caveat: the Binius import cone is still not validated. The Git index currently has active `UU` state in Binius files, and `lake build ArkLib.ProofSystem.Binius.BinaryBasefold.ReductionLogic` fails earlier in `Basic.lean` on `i.succ`/`i.castSucc` transport and relay-preservation equivalences. So these bridge lemmas are promising local structure, not a green #317 closeout yet.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body: **Lane note (~11:00Z):** I have the **general** (`steps`-inductive) discharges of both `PreTensorCombineMultilinearResidual` and `FoldPreTensorCombineAffineSplitResidual` written and compiling in `Soundness/PreTensorMultilinear.lean` — the full multilinearity theorem `iterated_fold = multilinearCombine (preTensorCombine stack)` by last-step peel + MSB tensor recursion, and the affine even/odd split via first-step peel + LSB recursion + binary-indicator collapse. It subsumes the step-1 bridges noted above (those remain useful as independent sanity anchors). Landing after the in-flight rebuild of the `Incremental` cone completes. The per-fiber `Prop4212Case1` lane (building on the `fiberwiseDisagreementSetPerFiber` brick) remains with whoever claimed it — I also have the fiber-composition brick `qMap_total_fiber_succ_decompose` (MSB of the fiber index selects the top quotient level) in `FiberDependence.lean`, compiling now, which that lane will need for the witness extraction.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
## ✅ `PreTensorCombineMultilinearResidual` content PROVEN — and ⚠️ a laundering hazard in the in-flight tree

**Proven, machine-verified** (md5 `993b27bc…`, log clean, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`): `iterated_fold_eq_multilinearCombine_preTensorCombine` — the exact content of the residual, via: peel the first fold (`iterated_fold_first`), the fold-is-affine-in-the-challenge identity, LSB factoring (`multilinearCombine_recursive_form_first`), even/odd split, induction. The residual *class* was already deleted by a concurrent lane in favor of this direct lemma (no external consumers — API-safe). Verified copy preserved at `~/binius317_incremental_VERIFIED.lean`; landing is gated on reconciling with the actively-edited in-tree file (now a third md5).

**⚠️ Hazard found while verifying**: an intermediate in-tree draft (md5 `74bb9535…`) FAILS — a helper repeats an `omit [SampleableType L]` mistake → kernel timeout → the final lemma elaborates with `(kernel) unknown constant` and **`#print axioms` shows the target lemma depending on ITSELF as an axiom**. A "0 sorry" grep on such a file is misleading. Rule (worth adding to the wiki): trust only `#print axioms` on a file that compiles with exit 0.

Two of four #317 residuals now have proven content (`FoldMatrixDetNeZeroResidual` discharged; this one proven pending reconcile). Remaining: `FoldPreTensorCombineAffineSplitResidual` (partially built lane-side), `Prop4212Case1Residual`.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
**Claim + audit refresh (goal session 2026-06-10, afternoon lane).** Full-tree residual census at `a075a1b66` — the residual classes now in the Binius tree, with classification from a fresh multi-agent audit:

| Residual | Where | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `Prop4212Case1Residual` | Incremental.lean:230 | **No longer false-as-stated**: `foldingBadEvent`/`incrementalFoldingBadEvent` were migrated to the honest `fiberwiseDisagreementSetPerFiber` (Compliance.lean). Provable as stated; scratch prover running now (needs one new brick: bottom-level fiber peel `qMap_total_fiber_succ_peel_first`, provable via `qMap_total_fiber_repr_coeff` + basis-repr injectivity; per-y affine SZ + union bound mirror the retained comment body, with `Pr_le_Pr_of_implies` + `prob_uniform_eq_card_filter_div_card` from Data/Probability/Instances.lean). |
| `Prop4212Case2Residual` | Incremental.lean:1852 | Weld-distance: ALL five chain deps exist and are proven, including the DG25 proximity-gap layer (`ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/ProximityGap/DG25/` — 0 sorry, 0 axioms). Remaining: Lemma 4.22 fiber-projection distance step (= `PreTensorCombineJointProximityResidual`) and the `s = 0` boundary of the close→affine-line bridge (`[NeZero steps]` incompatibility at ϑ−(k+1)=0). |
| `PreTensorCombineJointProximityResidual` | Incremental.lean:1659 | Distance counting through the fold's fiber projection; medium. |
| `Prop421Case1FiberwiseCloseResidual` | Proposition4_21.lean:68 | Audit: provable as stated via the all-steps matrix evaluator `single_point_localized_fold_matrix_form_eq_iterated_fold` (Prelude.lean:2132) + `foldMatrix_det_ne_zero` (FoldDetDischarge.lean) + ONE new lemma: tensor bit-order bridge `(challengeTensorProduct n r).get idx = challengeTensorExpansion n (r ∘ Fin.rev) idx`. |
| `Prop421Case2FiberwiseFarResidual` | Proposition4_21.lean:440 | Same DG25 chain as the incremental Case 2, telescoped; audit re-running. |
| `FoldPreservesBBFCodeMembershipResidual` | Code.lean:1039 | Provable (DP24 Lemma 4.13 corollary); gap = general-level-i novel-basis reconstruction (degree/triangularity of `intermediateNovelBasisX` — CompPoly has the defs, no degree lemmas). Medium; resurrection target = commented legacy proof at Code.lean:941-1004 + `fold_advances_evaluation_poly_step` (PROVEN). |
| `PreviousSuffixFiberAlignmentResidual` | QueryPhasePrelims.lean:571 | EASY: complete old proof exists at QueryPhase.lean:317-395 (`iteratedQuotientMap_eq_qMap_total_fiber_extractMiddleFinMask`), just written against the outdated CompPoly `iteratedQuotientMap` signature — port it. |
| `ExtractMLPCorrectnessResidual` | Relations.lean:239 | Berlekamp–Welch decoder correctness + sDomain enumeration glue; medium plumbing. |

Cross-cutting findings worth recording: (a) `fiberwiseDistance`/`pair_fiberwiseDistance` in Code.lean are literal `:= 0` stubs and `fiberwiseClose` is definitionally plain UDR-closeness — a deliberate paper divergence (DP24's case split uses fiberwise distance) that the audit flags for a faithfulness note; (b) the legacy degenerate `fiberwiseDisagreementSet` still has live uses in 8 files though the events have migrated — consolidation candidate after Case 1 lands. Proving lanes are running; bricks will be pushed individually with build+axiom audits.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
**Lane claim (~14:05Z) + Case 2 demolition map.** Taking `Prop421Case1FiberwiseCloseResidual` (`Soundness/Proposition4_21.lean`) — NOT touching `Soundness/Incremental.lean`, `Code.lean`, or the `PreTensor*.lean` suite (active lane, build running as I write).

## Finding: Prop 4.21 Case 2 needs NO new math — the whole DG25 chain is already proven in-tree

While mapping the fiberwise-far branch against DP24 §4.4, I traced what `Prop421Case2FiberwiseFarResidual` actually needs. DP24's own proof closes Case 2 via **Theorem 2.4 = DG25 Corollary 1** (tensor-style proximity gap within unique decoding, error `ϑ·n/|L|`). Status of that chain in our tree, all **0-sorry**:

1. **Base case** (BCIKS20 Thm 4.1, RS affine-line CA within UDR): `ProximityGap.RS_correlatedAgreement_affineLines_uniqueDecodingRegime`, consumed by the proven `ahiv17_*_uniqueDecodingRegime` family in `ProximityGap/AHIV22.lean`.
2. **DG25 Thm 3.1** (affine gaps lift to interleaved codes): `affine_gaps_lifted_to_interleaved_codes` in `ProximityGap/DG25/MainResults.lean` — proven.
3. **Thm 3.6 (AER24)** (interleaved affine gaps ⟹ tensor gaps): `interleaved_affine_gaps_imply_tensor_gaps` in `DG25/Basic.lean` — proven.
4. **Cor 3.7** (RS tensor gaps within UDR, `ε = n`): `reedSolomon_multilinearCorrelatedAgreement_Nat` in `DG25/ReedSolomon.lean` — proven, welds 1–3.

And `BBF_Code` (`BinaryBasefold/Code.lean:64`) **is definitionally `ReedSolomon.code`** over the `sDomain`, so Cor 3.7 applies directly — no code-transport lemma needed beyond unfolding.

**What Case 2 actually still needs** (wiring, not math):
- Lemma 4.22 far-lift (the `PreTensor*` lane in flight — `jointProximityNat` against the interleaved destination code);
- the fold-as-tensor-combination bridge (already proven: `iterated_fold_eq_multilinearCombine_preTensorCombine`);
- a probability-form bridge from `Pr_{r ←$ᵖ (Fin steps → L)}[d(fold f r, C) ≤ UDR]` to `δ_ε_multilinearCorrelatedAgreement_Nat`'s contrapositive (the DP24 bound `steps·|S_next|/|L|` matches `ϑ·n/|L|` with `n = |S_next|` exactly).

## Case 1 plan (my lane)

DP24's Case 1 proof (p.40, eq. 39): per `y ∈ Δ_perFiber`, the folded difference at `y` is `⟨⊗ⱼ(1−rⱼ, rⱼ), M_y·(v_f − v_f̄)⟩`; `M_y` nonsingular (now in-tree: `foldMatrix_det_ne_zero`, `FoldDetSplit.lean`) + `y` disagreeing ⟹ the coefficient vector `a := M_y·Δv ≠ 0` ⟹ the ϑ-variate multilinear polynomial `s` has cube values exactly `a` ⟹ `s ≠ 0` ⟹ Schwartz–Zippel (`prob_schwartz_zippel_mv_polynomial`, `Data/Probability/Instances.lean`) gives `≤ steps/|L|` per fiber; union bound over `Δ_perFiber ⊆ S_next`. The single-point bridge `single_point_localized_fold_matrix_form_eq_iterated_fold` is proven in the working tree (`Prelude.lean:2588`). Working in a NEW file `Soundness/Prop421Case1Discharge.lean` to avoid collisions. Will report build + axiom audit.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body:
**Lane claim (goal session 2026-06-10, ~20:10Z lane D).** Taking the two unclaimed residual surfaces NOT covered by the three active lanes (ReductionLogic / Prop4_21 Case 1 / PreTensor+Incremental suite):

1. `PreviousSuffixFiberAlignmentResidual` (`Soundness/QueryPhasePrelims.lean:571`) — the `iteratedQuotientMap` ↔ `qMap_total_fiber` basis-coefficient alignment at block source `j·ϑ`. Plan: re-mechanize the coefficient computation against the current CompPoly API, working around the `0 + k` defeq divergence with explicit `Fin.cast` transport lemmas in a NEW file (`Soundness/SuffixFiberAlignment.lean`) to avoid touching actively-edited files.
2. `ExtractMLPCorrectnessResidual` (`Relations.lean:239`) — Berlekamp–Welch extraction iff-UDR-close at base level. Plan: survey the in-tree `BerlekampWelch.decoder` correctness lemmas, build the `sDomain` point-enumeration equiv glue, land the instance in a NEW file.

Also picking up the #313 front-door hypothesis audit (CoreInteractionPhase/General/FRIBinius/BBFSmallFieldIOPCS wrapper hypotheses + RingSwitching composition) as the closeout lane — will post the audit table there. Not touching: ReductionLogic.lean, Proposition4_21.lean, Incremental.lean, Code.lean, PreTensor*.lean, FiberDependence.lean.

title: Discharge Binius Residual Axioms (Unproven Props)
